svg.title: x86 Boot Sequence Flowchart
svg.desc: Flowchart showing the full boot sequence from power button press to bootloader execution in real mode

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What physically happens when a power button is pressed on a computer?')
sendPrompt('What is the x86 reset vector at 0xFFFFFFF0 and why is it hardwired?')
sendPrompt('What is firmware ROM and how is it stored on the motherboard?')
sendPrompt('What does POST (Power-On Self Test) check and what happens if it fails?')
g.text: Verifies RAM, CPU and connected devices

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What happens when POST fails, beep codes and error handling')
sendPrompt('How does BIOS firmware search for bootable devices in order?')
g.text: USB, hard disk, CD in configured order

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Why does the firmware read exactly 512 bytes from the start of a device?')
g.text: Checks last two bytes for boot signature

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is the MBR boot signature 0x55AA and why are those specific bytes used?')
sendPrompt('What is the Master Boot Record structure and how is it laid out?')
g.text: 512 bytes loaded, this is our bootloader

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is x86 real mode, memory limits, no protection, BIOS interrupts?')
g.text: CPU jumps to 0x7C00, real mode begins
